summary: Iteration 7 — frontend-only verification of three new enhancements: (1) in-game Settings panel (gear icon replaces top-bar 'Sound' button) with mute + volume slider + Journal text-size selector, (2) chapter-specific ambient lifecycle for ch1/ch4 ('wasteland'/'road'), and (3) lazy-loaded ThresholdCard via React.lazy + Suspense. All 11 user-facing acceptance checks PASSED. ONE spec deviation found in sound.js: 'wasteland' and 'road' ambient variants are NOT explicitly implemented — they silently fall through to the default branch, so the audible ambient for chapters 1/2/4 does NOT match the spec (G1 49Hz / lowpass 180Hz / 45%-intensity heartbeats for wasteland; E2 82.4Hz + B3 + detuned + lowpass 700Hz + LFO + _roadChime every 12-20s for road). The lifecycle is safe (no errors on chapter switches), playHeartbeat(intensity) is implemented, and _roadChime() helper exists — but neither is wired into startAmbient(). Lazy-load split verified: webpack emits a separate chunk 'src_components_ThresholdCard_jsx.chunk.js' that is loaded only on /threshold; fallback data-testid='threshold-card-fallback' is visible immediately after navigation and is replaced by data-testid='threshold-card'. Download still produces 'heartbound-threshold-mira.png'. No console errors, no pageerror across /game/{1..5} + /threshold + /journal/1 + /map.

integration_issues.issue: startAmbient('wasteland') and startAmbient('road') fall through to the 'default' branch in /app/frontend/src/game/sound.js. The spec describes distinct sound beds for these variants (wasteland: G1 49Hz sine + 98Hz overtone, lowpass 180Hz Q=0.3, faint heartbeat every 14–22s at 45% intensity; road: E2 82.4Hz + detuned twin + B3 246.9Hz triangle, lowpass 700Hz with slow LFO, _roadChime every 12–20s). _roadChime() is defined but never called. playHeartbeat() now accepts an intensity param but no scheduler invokes it at 0.45 for wasteland. Result: chapters 1/2/4 share the same default A1+A2 bed used elsewhere instead of their unique beds.

action_items: Add explicit `if (variant === 'wasteland')` and `if (variant === 'road')` branches inside startAmbient() in /app/frontend/src/game/sound.js. Implement the wasteland bed (G1 49Hz sine + 98Hz overtone, lowpass 180Hz Q=0.3, schedule playHeartbeat(0.45) on a 14000-22000ms randomized interval). Implement the road bed (E2 82.4Hz + detuned twin via .detune offset, B3 246.9Hz triangle, lowpass 700Hz with an LFO modulating cutoff for movement, schedule _roadChime() on a 12000-20000ms randomized interval).
Update the JSDoc above startAmbient (line ~193): 'Variants: "default" | "cave" | "fear"' should include 'wasteland' and 'road'.
critical_code_review_comments: sound.js — startAmbient() has no branches for 'wasteland' or 'road'. They silently fall through to the 'default' bed. _roadChime() is defined but unreferenced (dead code). playHeartbeat() accepts intensity but no caller passes 0.45 anywhere — the new intensity contract is wired into the signature but not exercised by the chapter ambient.

GameScreen.jsx — Each chapter mount fires startAmbient(...) then on unmount stopAmbient(). _stopAmbientInternal ramps the old bus to 0 over 1.2s and the new bus ramps up over 3s, which gives a crossfade-like overlap. OK in practice, but the 'default' fall-through means the spec-promised cross-variant audible difference between chapters 1↔3↔4↔5 isn't actually present for 1 and 4 (both currently sound like the default bed).

SettingsPanel.jsx — The 'click outside to close' handler attaches a `mousedown` listener after a 0ms timeout to avoid re-closing on the opening click; this is correct and works. One small consideration: the listener doesn't ignore clicks on the gear button itself, so clicking the gear while the panel is open will close it (via outside-click) AND then re-toggle via onOpenSettings — which produces the desired toggle behavior, but only by accident. Consider gating with a ref or stopping propagation on the trigger for clarity.
SettingsPanel.jsx — accent-amber-300 + disabled:opacity-40 on the range input look correct; aria-pressed on the segmented size buttons is implemented; data-testids match spec exactly.

ThresholdScreen.jsx — lazy() + Suspense fallback works as intended; the chunk file `src_components_ThresholdCard_jsx.chunk.js` is split out of the main bundle and loaded only when /threshold is visited.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of acceptance checks; 1 spec deviation in audio implementation

test_credentials: none required (no auth in this app)
seed_data_creation: localStorage['heartbound.save.v1'] = JSON.stringify({hero:{preset:'arin',name:'Mira',accent:'ember'}, lantern:0.7, currentChapter:1, completedChapters:[1,2,3,4,5], journal:{}, unlockedQualities:[], mirrorLensUnlocked:true, mirrorLensTooltipShown:true, journalTextSize:'md', hasSave:true}); also cleared 'heartbound.audio.muted' and 'heartbound.audio.volume' to test from a clean state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Verified user-facing checks: settings-button replaces removed 'Sound' button; panel opens; mute toggles update localStorage 'heartbound.audio.muted' to 'true'/'false'; volume slider is disabled when muted and shows '—'; React-friendly slider update via Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(HTMLInputElement.prototype,'value').set + dispatchEvent works and writes float string to 'heartbound.audio.volume' (saw '0.55' for value=55); reload preserves both slider value and journalTextSize selection; /journal/1 with journalTextSize='lg' applies text-3xl md:text-4xl to journal-prompt and text-2xl to journal-input; Escape, X button, and outside-click all dismiss the panel; /game/{1,2,3,4,5} all load with no console errors; /threshold shows the fallback briefly then the card and downloads 'heartbound-threshold-mira.png'; webpack chunk 'src_components_ThresholdCard_jsx.chunk.js' is split out. The audio-spec deviation (wasteland/road variants missing in startAmbient) is the only outstanding item — it does not break the UI or throw at runtime; it only means the ambient bed for chapters 1/2/4 audibly matches the 'default' variant instead of the spec'd unique beds.
rca of the issue: sound.js startAmbient() switches on variant string — branches exist for 'cave', 'fear', and 'default' (else). The chapter→variant mapping in GameScreen.jsx is {1:'wasteland', 2:'wasteland', 3:'cave', 4:'road', 5:'fear'} — so when chapters 1, 2, or 4 mount, startAmbient receives 'wasteland' or 'road', neither of which matches a branch, so execution falls into the else block which constructs the default A1+A2 bed. The helper _roadChime() and the intensity parameter on playHeartbeat() were added in anticipation of the new variants but never invoked from startAmbient. Mitigation: add two explicit `else if` branches inside startAmbient (before the default else) implementing the wasteland and road beds per the original spec, scheduling playHeartbeat(0.45) at 14000-22000ms for wasteland and _roadChime() at 12000-20000ms for road.
